description: The cat: the household's self-possessed companion — affectionate on its own schedule, silent on its feet, entirely finished as a creature. The second of the companion animals coined by owner ruling, and the one that would have demanded it eventually anyway.
sound_symbolism: Small 'mi', hushed 'she', self-contained 'ko' — a word that enters the room the way its animal does: quietly, and on its own terms.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'misheko mua nuwera nulae.' (The cat sleeps on the bed.) 'misheko maeli thalo.' (The cat walks quietly.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Every meditation tradition eventually notices the cat: present, unhurried, complete in itself — the practice, furred.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The granary hired the cat and the household kept it; grain storage and cats are a single invention told as two.
